channel.title: Comments for page "Commentary on Selected Items in the Seimei Gallery"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Commentary on Selected Items in the Seimei Gallery"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. Everyone should read it and upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the characterization you've got going for this incarnation of IJAMEA. The subtle hinting towards their unique position between the Imperial Court and the military, their struggle to wrangle paratech clinically while maintaining reverence for the items involved and (my favorite) the rivalry they had going with one of Japan's most powerful feudal warlords really sold me on this. Not much else to say, other than it's an effective, enjoyable piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad the case files inspired you! This is a fun little thing and I liked it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig the heck out of this. :) And the presentation is dry and formal without being dull, good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed. Much better than hearing the mumblings of a bunch of people debating over the objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the changes you've made, feels more wholistic and immersive.

item.title: Thanks, Attribution, and Others
item.content:encoded:
Allow me to express appreciations to Modern_Erasmus, Tufto, and Dr0Shadow for having read through this before.

This was inspired by UIU's Case Files, modified for IJAMEA to pick apart objects from Japanese history.

Articles mentioned in this article include:

  • SCP-1000 (Bigfoot) by thedeadlymoose
  • SCP-2953 (Polymorphism-Inducing Rocks) by MrWrong
  • Golden Horde Blues by Vezaz
  • Document-2481 by SunnyClockwork

Now, for some translation matters：

+ show block
– hide block

Master Gozen = 悟善大師
tennin (heavenly person) = 天人
Tennin (heavenly benevolence) = 天仁
Amatsuko = 天子
Amatsu-miyako = 天京 (heavenly capital)

Others:

  • Listing is chronological
  • I'm using Japanese era names (even if almost no one would figure out the real year, unless they Googled it) for the sake of immersion. And it makes little sense to have the years be the BCE/CE calendar in a source that is written before the system was introduced to the setting.
  • With the exception of Amatsuko and Master Gozen, all other named persons are historic figures.
